sanskrit:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः।
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः॥
transliteration: adharma-abhibhavāt kṛṣṇa praduṣyanti kula-striyaḥ | strīṣu duṣṭāsu vārṣṇeya jāyate varṇa-saṅkaraḥ ||
literal_meaning: When irreligion predominates, O Kṛṣṇa, the women of the family become corrupted; from the corruption of women, O descendant of Vṛṣṇi, unwanted progeny is born.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices a catastrophic fear that moral collapse within a society will first infect its most protected spaces—its women—thereby eroding the emotional and cultural womb from which future generations emerge. Psychologically, he projects societal doom onto the feminine ‘other,’ revealing his own terror of boundary loss, identity diffusion, and the shame of failing to safeguard what he idealizes as pure.
psychological_state: A hyper-aroused catastrophizer who equates change with contamination and feels personally responsible for preventing imagined cultural extinction.
trigger_scenario: A father scrolling social media, seeing his teenage daughter adopt values he labels ‘woke,’ and suddenly fearing the family lineage is dissolving into moral chaos.
anchor_text: Protect the integrity of home and future by guarding the moral climate today.
